# Implementation Plan: GrantGenie Core

**Branch**: `001-grantgenie-core` | **Date**: 2026-07-03 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)

**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-grantgenie-core/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command. See `.specify/templates/plan-template.md` for the execution workflow.

## Summary

GrantGenie is a multi-tenant SaaS that helps small nonprofits discover relevant grant opportunities and draft funder-aligned proposals grounded in their own approved materials. The system delivers four P1 capabilities: (1) **discovery** of grants via a daily corpus crawl with boolean eligibility matching against the org profile, (2) **org profile + boilerplate library** that ingests PDF/DOCX/TXT/MD documents into a pgvector-backed RAG store, (3) **proposal drafting** via a cost-aware multi-model AI service with citations and automated eval gates, and (4) **reviewer collaboration** with author-held edit locks and inline comments. Defer to P2/P3: budget narrative helper, deadline tracker, funder-specific tailoring, reviewer invitation flow. The architecture follows Clean Architecture / DDD per the project constitution, with a Laravel 11 backend, Angular 18 frontend, Python 3.12+ AI service, PostgreSQL+pgvector, Redis cache, and containerized deployment to AKS via Terraform.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**:
- Backend: PHP 8.3+ / Laravel 11 (constitution-mandated)
- Frontend: Angular 18 (TypeScript, standalone components) (constitution-mandated)
- AI Service: Python 3.12+ (constitution-mandated)

**Primary Dependencies**:
- Backend: Laravel 11, Eloquent, Laravel Passport or Sanctum, spatie/laravel-permission (RBAC), league/csv (import/export), OpenTelemetry PHP SDK, ramsey/uuid, predis/predis
- AI Service: FastAPI, pydantic v2, langchain or llama-index, OpenAI Python SDK, Anthropic Python SDK, tiktoken, pgvector-python, sentence-transformers (for local embeddings fallback)
- Frontend: Angular 18 (standalone), RxJS, Angular Signals, NG-ZORRO or Angular Material (TBD in research), openid-client-js, Monaco editor (proposal editing)
- Cross-cutting: Redis, NATS or Redis Streams (event bus), OpenTelemetry Collector

**Storage**:
- PostgreSQL 16 + pgvector extension (primary store + vector embeddings)
- Redis 7 (idempotency, query cache, session, rate-limit counters, edit-lock state)
- S3-compatible object storage (boilerplate documents) — MinIO in dev, Azure Blob in prod
- Daily-rotated JSON logs to `storage/logs/<service>/YYYY-MM-DD.json` (constitution Principle V)

**Testing**:
- Backend: Pest 2 (PHPUnit successor), Laravel architecture tests, Mockery
- AI Service: pytest, pytest-asyncio, schemathesis (contract), deepeval / RAGAS (eval gates)
- Frontend: Jest + Angular Testing Library, Playwright (E2E), Storybook (visual regression)
- Cross-cutting: Testcontainers (Postgres+pgvector, Redis), WireMock (external APIs), k6 (load)

**Target Platform**:
- Containerized Linux services running on AKS (Azure Kubernetes Service)
- Local dev: docker-compose stack (Laravel artisan serve, Angular dev server, Python uvicorn, Postgres+pgvector, Redis, MinIO)
- CI: GitHub Actions runners (ubuntu-latest)

**Project Type**: Multi-component web application (Option 2) — Laravel API backend, Angular SPA frontend, Python AI service. Each is a separate deployable bounded context.

**Performance Goals**:
- Grant discovery p95 < 5 s (SC-001) — incl. eligibility filter + pagination
- Proposal draft end-to-end p95 < 6 s (SC-002) — first-token < 1.5 s (constitution Principle III), streaming response
- AI eval gates latency budget: relevance/faithfulness scoring completes within the 6 s window
- Read-path API p95 < 200 ms (constitution Principle III)
- Write-path API p95 < 400 ms (constitution Principle III)
- RAG retrieval top-k=5 chunks in < 300 ms p95

**Constraints**:
- Multi-tenant isolation mandatory: tenant key on every table, row-level security in Postgres, no cross-tenant query path (constitution Principle IV)
- AI cost ceiling per tenant per month tracked in FinOps dashboard (constitution Principle V)
- GPU scale-to-zero for the AI service when idle (constitution Principle V)
- Prompt-injection defense and PII redaction at the AI service boundary (constitution Principle IV)
- No secrets in code or images; OIDC/OAuth2 auth; RBAC scoped per tenant
- Mobile is out of scope for MVP (responsive web only — PWA enhancement possible later)
- 99.9% availability for read operations (SC-004)

**Scale/Scope**:
- MVP target: 1,000 nonprofit tenants, ~5 users each = 5,000 MAU
- Grant corpus: ~50,000 active grants, refreshed daily (cumulative < 200k rows including historical)
- Boilerplate documents: avg 20 docs per tenant, avg 30 pages each = ~600 chunks per tenant
- Proposal volume: ~3 proposals per tenant per month = ~3,000/month, ~36k/year
- AI token budget: ~50k input + 20k output tokens per proposal draft, 5x markup from RAG context
- Storage estimate (Y1): ~50 GB for documents, ~10 GB for embeddings, ~5 GB for structured data

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| Principle | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| I. Spec-First Development | PASS | Spec exists at `specs/001-grantgenie-core/spec.md`; 18 functional requirements, 7 success criteria, 7 user stories; traceability IDs (`FR-NNN`, `SC-NNN`) will be referenced by tasks, tests, code. |
| II. Clean Architecture & DDD | PASS | Three bounded contexts: `Identity & Tenancy`, `Grant Discovery`, `Proposal Drafting`. Each owns its data, language, and domain logic. Anti-corruption layer between Laravel backend and external AI service. PHPStan max + Laravel architecture tests will enforce inward dependency direction. |
| III. Test-Driven & Eval-Gated Quality | PASS | TDD enforced for every behavioral change; AI eval gates (relevance ≥ 0.85, faithfulness ≥ 0.90) gate surface; performance budgets enforced in CI. Eval framework = RAGAS for retrieval, deepeval for generation. |
| IV. Security & Multi-Tenant Isolation | PASS | OIDC/OAuth2 via Auth0; RBAC scoped per tenant (4 roles: Admin, Writer, Reviewer, Viewer); Postgres RLS for tenant isolation; prompt-injection defense (system-prompt hardening + output filtering) and PII redaction at AI service boundary; no secrets in code/images. |
| V. Observability & Cost Awareness | PASS | OpenTelemetry tracing across all services; structured JSON logs with correlation IDs, rotated daily; cost-aware AI router (cheapest adequate model first, fallback to higher tier); FinOps dashboard for per-tenant cost; GPU scale-to-zero for AI service. |
| Technology Stack | PASS | All mandated technologies adopted: PHP 8.3+/Laravel 11 (backend), Angular 18 (frontend), Python 3.12+ (AI), PostgreSQL+pgvector, Redis, Docker+K8s(AKS)+Terraform, GitHub Actions. |
| Development Workflow | PASS | Plan is the second of four Spec Kit gates (spec → plan → tasks → implement). Review gate at end of this plan. |

**No violations** — proceed to Phase 0 research.

### Post-design re-evaluation

| Principle | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| I. Spec-First Development | PASS | All 18 FRs and 7 SCs remain traceable; new artifacts (`data-model.md`, `contracts/`, `quickstart.md`) reference them directly. |
| II. Clean Architecture & DDD | PASS | `data-model.md` defines 5 bounded contexts with clear ownership; `contracts/api-openapi.yaml` and `contracts/ai-service-http.yaml` form the anti-corruption layer between backend and AI service; `contracts/events.md` defines the cross-context event spine. |
| III. Test-Driven & Eval-Gated Quality | PASS | `quickstart.md` §7 maps each SC to a concrete validation command; eval-gate threshold (SC-003) is the gating condition for `proposal.status` transition in `data-model.md`. |
| IV. Security & Multi-Tenant Isolation | PASS | RLS policies documented per-table; AI service input/output safety contract in `contracts/ai-service-http.yaml`; OIDC, RBAC, and idempotency keys reflected in OpenAPI security schemes. |
| V. Observability & Cost Awareness | PASS | Every event in `events.md` includes `correlation_id`; AI cost (`cost_cents`) recorded per proposal in `data-model.md` and emitted in `proposal.draft.completed` event; k6 smoke tests in `quickstart.md` enforce SC-001/002. |

**No new violations introduced by Phase 1 design.** Plan is complete.

## Project Structure

specs/001-grantgenie-core/
├── plan.md              # This file (/speckit.plan command output)

│   ├── ai-service-grpc.proto  # OR ai-service-http.yaml

└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks command - NOT created by /speckit.plan)

### Source Code (repository root)

Three deployable bounded contexts, each with Clean Architecture layering:

```text
backend/                          # Laravel 11 API
├── app/
│   ├── Domain/                   # Entities, Value Objects, Domain Events (no Laravel deps)
│   │   ├── Identity/             #   Tenant, User, Role
│   │   ├── Grant/                #   Grant, EligibilityRule, EligibilityDecision
│   │   ├── OrgProfile/           #   OrgProfile, BoilerplateDocument
│   │   └── Proposal/             #   Proposal, ProposalSection, ReviewComment, EditLock
│   ├── Application/              # Use cases, DTOs, ports (interfaces)
│   │   ├── Identity/
│   │   ├── Grant/
│   │   ├── OrgProfile/
│   │   └── Proposal/
│   ├── Infrastructure/           # Adapters: Eloquent, Redis, Http clients, Storage
│   │   ├── Persistence/          #   Eloquent repos
│   │   ├── Http/                 #   Controllers, Middleware, Form Requests
│   │   ├── External/             #   AI service client, OIDC client, Crawler
│   │   └── Cache/
│   └── Providers/
├── config/
├── database/
│   ├── migrations/
│   ├── factories/
│   └── seeders/
├── routes/
│   ├── api.php
│   └── console.php
├── tests/
│   ├── Unit/
│   ├── Feature/
│   ├── Architecture/             # PHPStan / arch tests
│   └── Integration/
├── composer.json
├── phpstan.neon
└── Dockerfile

frontend/                         # Angular 18 SPA
├── src/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── core/                 # Singletons: AuthInterceptor, ApiClient, Guards
│   │   ├── shared/               # Reusable components, pipes, directives
│   │   ├── features/
│   │   │   ├── auth/
│   │   │   ├── org-profile/
│   │   │   ├── boilerplate/
│   │   │   ├── discovery/
│   │   │   ├── proposals/
│   │   │   ├── review/
│   │   │   ├── tracking/
│   │   │   └── admin/
│   │   └── app.routes.ts
│   ├── assets/
│   ├── environments/
│   └── styles/
├── e2e/                          # Playwright
├── package.json
├── angular.json
└── Dockerfile

ai-service/                       # Python 3.12+ FastAPI
├── src/
│   └── grantgenie_ai/
│       ├── api/                  # FastAPI routers
│       ├── core/                 # Config, logging, telemetry
│       ├── domain/               # Models, citations, eval scoring
│       ├── retrieval/            # RAG: chunking, embedding, search (pgvector)
│       ├── generation/           # Multi-model router, prompt templates
│       ├── eval/                 # RAGAS/deepeval gates
│       └── safety/               # Prompt-injection defense, PII redaction
├── tests/
│   ├── unit/
│   ├── integration/              # Testcontainers Postgres+pgvector
│   └── eval/                     # Eval dataset + threshold checks
├── pyproject.toml
└── Dockerfile

infra/                            # Terraform for AKS
├── modules/
│   ├── aks/
│   ├── postgres-flexible/
│   ├── redis-cache/
│   ├── blob-storage/
│   ├── ai-gpu-node-pool/
│   └── networking/
├── envs/
│   ├── dev/
│   ├── staging/
│   └── prod/
└── README.md

.github/
└── workflows/
    ├── ci-backend.yml
    ├── ci-frontend.yml
    ├── ci-ai-service.yml
    ├── eval-gates.yml
    └── deploy.yml

docker-compose.yml                # Local dev stack
docker-compose.override.yml
Makefile
README.md
```

**Structure Decision**: Web application with three independently deployable services (backend, frontend, ai-service) plus a shared `infra/` Terraform module. Each service follows Clean Architecture's inward-dependency rule, enforced by PHPStan max + Laravel architecture tests on the backend and import-linter on the AI service. Bounded contexts are owned by each service: backend = Identity + Grant + OrgProfile + Proposal, AI service = Retrieval + Generation + Eval + Safety (anti-corruption layer between them).
